label: BREAKING
hook: FED: NO RATE CUTS EXPECTED IN 2026
stat1: Less than a 1-in-2 chance of any 2026 cut
stat2: 4 Fed members voted against the decision
stat3: Biggest internal Fed split in 33 years
caption:
The Fed held rates today. Markets called it hawkish.

Worth pausing on this:

The same market pricing only 44% odds of any rate cuts in 2026 is the same market that priced seven cuts in early 2024. They got one.

Four FOMC members dissented today — the highest count since 1992. That's not a unified hawkish front. That's the Fed openly disagreeing with itself in public.

When markets and the Fed are both confused at the same time, "consensus" doesn't exist. The story is reactivity, not strategy.

— The Clam Letter
